title: ewing — Quantitative Population Ethology (QPE) Simulation Package
text: ewing R package implements Bland Ewing’s event-driven individual-based simulation host-parasite ecological systems. individual organism tracked life cycle using competing risks stage transitions, spatial movement triangular substrate grid. Version: 1.9.8 Author: Brian S. Yandell (yandell@stat.wisc.edu) License: GPL-3 GitHub: https://github.com/byandell/ewing Live Shiny app: https://connect.doit.wisc.edu/SystemsEthology

title: ewing — Quantitative Population Ethology (QPE) Simulation Package
ewing — Quantitative Population Ethology (QPE) Simulation Package
code: library(ewing)  # Initialize simulation (default: 200 hosts, 100 parasites) mysim <- init.simulation()  # Run 4000 steps simres <- future.events(mysim, nstep = 4000, refresh = 200, plotit = FALSE)  # Visualize plot(simres)                       # Age classes and substrate distribution ggplot_current(simres, "host")     # Spatial positions of hosts temp.plot(simres)                  # Temperature/degree-day relationship  # Multiple runs and envelopes mysims <- ewing_discrete(nsim = 10, nstep = 4000, verbose = TRUE) ggplot_ewing_envelopes(mysims, confidence = TRUE)  # Pre-computed example data data(simdata) ggplot_ewing_envelopes(simdata)  # Launch interactive Shiny app ewingApp()

title: ewing — Quantitative Population Ethology (QPE) Simulation Package
text: Required sheets configuration workbook: - organism.features — species, life stages, reproduction, parasitism type - future.{species} — competing risks stage transitions (one per species) - {host}.{parasite} — interaction rates - substrate.{species} — movement weights per species - substrate.substrate — substrate connectivity - temperature.base, temperature.par — thermal regime (optional)

title: ewing — Quantitative Population Ethology (QPE) Simulation Package
text: ewing — main simulation result (community + metadata) ewing_discrete — discretized multi-run results ewing_envelopes / ewing_envelope — envelope objects multiple runs ewing_ageclass / ewing_substrate / ewing_snapshot — intermediate plot data tricoord — tridiagonal coordinate vector (+/- operators) substrate — geometric substrate network

title: ewing — Quantitative Population Ethology (QPE) Simulation Package
text: Competing risks: Multiple possible stage transitions given life stage, drawn future.host/future.parasite tables Leftist tree event queue: Binary tree structure O(log n) event scheduling per species Triangular coordinate system: Substrate represented interconnected triangles; coordinates (, b, c) c = -(+b) Degree-day integration: Species timing can use clock hours (hr) degree-days (DD); spline interpolation converts

title: Module Architecture & Reactive Flow
text: coordinating server loop ewingServer() maps interactions explicitly:
code: ewingServer <- function(id) {   shiny::moduleServer(id, function(input, output, session) {     ns <- session$ns          # 1. Initialize Parameters Table & Species Models     init_par <- initParServer("init_par", simres = shiny::reactive({ tryCatch(sim_data$simres(), error=function(e) NULL) }))          # 2. Main Run Simulation      sim_data <- simServer("sim", init_par)          # 3. Dynamic Visualizations (Evaluating on active state)     dist_plot <- distPlotServer("dist_plot", sim_data$simres)     spp_plot <- substrateServer("substrate", sim_data$simres)     env_plot <- envPlotServer("env_plot", sim_data$simres, sim_data$nsim)          # 4. Bind the File Extractor     downloadServer("download", sim_data = sim_data, distplot = dist_plot, sppplot = spp_plot, envplot = env_plot)     ...   }) }

title: Module Architecture & Reactive Flow
text: user interface uses bslib::page_sidebar() build split-frame responsive layout: - Global Sidebar: Contains panels initParInput(), simInput(), distPlotInput(), envPlotInput(), downloadInput(). - Dynamic Main Panel: active tabs change dynamically based value nsim chosen user.
code: # Dynamic tab builder in R/ewingApp.R output$dynamic_tabs <- shiny::renderUI({   sims <- shiny::req(sim_data$nsim())   if (sims == 1) {     bslib::navset_tab(       bslib::nav_panel("Dist Plots", distPlotOutput(ns("dist_plot"))),       bslib::nav_panel("Substrate Plots", bslib::card(substrateOutput(ns("substrate")))),       bslib::nav_panel("Input Data", initParOutput(ns("init_par")))     )   } else {     bslib::navset_tab(       bslib::nav_panel("Envelope Plots", bslib::card(envPlotOutput(ns("env_plot")))),       bslib::nav_panel("Input Data", initParOutput(ns("init_par")))     )   } })

title: Developer Guide Overview
text: package source files reside inside R/ (modular source functions Shiny sub-modules) inst/ (external metadata, configurations, scripts).

title: Developer Guide Overview
text: center simulation run S3 class object type community, created init.simulation(). community structure comprises: - pop: matrix species storing individual attributes. Rows feature attributes, columns map individuals O(n). - org: Organism feature thresholds rates. - temp: Environmental temperature degree-day spline thresholds. - count: Historical counts logs. detailed information state matrices, see dataorg.md.

title: Developer Guide Overview
text: default parameters connectivity tables stored data/ folder: - default.xlsx: complete baseline spreadsheet containing configuration parameters (features, risks, transitions, substrates, temperature splines). - simdata.rda: Pre-computed discrete simulation runs used envelope testing. - Flat .txt files: Single-sheet tabular versions parameters used automated unit tests (e.g. organism.features.txt, future.host.txt).

title: Developer Guide Overview
text: Click links explore specific technical areas codebase: Module Architecture & Flow Understand high-level layout, modular communication using reactive interfaces, central download router framework. Simulation & Parameters Setup Examine parameter worksheets parsed (initParApp.R) simulations stepped forward (simApp.R). Dynamic Visualizations Trace age class plotting (distPlotApp.R), spatial triangular grids (substrateApp.R), confidence envelope bounds (envPlotApp.R). Utility & Companion Modules Explore file exports (downloadApp.R), Leaflet GIS explorers (leafletApp.R), developer test applications (futureApp, initApp).

title: Developer Guide Overview
text: index Shiny app files inside R/ directory, mapped design roles:

title: Simulation & Parameters Configuration Modules
text: parameters panel, defined R/initParApp.R, allows researchers view default configuration matrices upload custom configurations.

title: Simulation & Parameters Configuration Modules
text: system reads parameters two ways: 1. Default Config: Falls back package-wide Excel workbook default.xlsx. 2. User Overwrite: Users can upload custom Excel workbook .xlsx containing required worksheets (e.g. organism.features, future.host, etc.).

title: Simulation & Parameters Configuration Modules
text: initParInput(): Renders sliders dynamically species starting sizes based organism spreadsheet (typically host parasite sizes range 0–500). initParUI(): Outputs list active input selections. initParOutput(): Displays selected workbook sheet using searchable, paginated DT::dataTableOutput.

title: Simulation & Parameters Configuration Modules
text: Reads worksheets Excel workbook using readxl library. Dynamically creates UI sliders species retrieved get.organisms(). Updates active data table output$org_table dynamically user changes sheet selection dropdown.

title: Simulation & Parameters Configuration Modules
text: simulation execution panel, defined R/simApp.R, manages simulation runs step-wise progression.

title: Simulation & Parameters Configuration Modules
text: Simulation steps: Total events count (1,000 10,000) multi-simulations. Number Simulations (nsim): Mode selector (1 single interactive runs, 10–200 confidence bounds). Steps per click: chunk size (10 500) advance single runs. Init / Run Button (go_init): Spawns initial community. Step Forward Button (go_step): Appears using shiny::conditionalPanel nsim == 1 simulation initialized.

title: Simulation & Parameters Configuration Modules
text: module server manages internal reactive value active_sim containing state. execution logic flows follows:
code: graph TD     ClickGo[User clicks Init / Run] --> CheckNsim{Is nsim == 1?}          CheckNsim -->|Yes: Single Run| InitSim[init.simulation]     InitSim --> AdvanceFirst[future.events step_size]     AdvanceFirst --> SetState[active_sim state updated]          CheckNsim -->|No: Multi Run| LoopDiscrete[Loop nsim times]     LoopDiscrete --> EDiscrete[ewing_discrete1 steps]     EDiscrete --> MakeDiscrete[make_ewing_discrete]     MakeDiscrete --> SetState          ClickStep[User clicks Step Forward] --> CheckInit{Is initialized?}     CheckInit -->|Yes| AdvanceMore[future.events active_sim step_size]     AdvanceMore --> SetState

title: Simulation & Parameters Configuration Modules
text: init.simulation(): Generates community structure. future.events(): Advances community state performing event queue pops executing competing risks event handlers (death, birth, movement). ewing_discrete1(): Fast C-like wrapper evaluating single runs build discrete bounds.

title: Utility & Companion Application Modules
text: Defined R/downloadApp.R, utility module bridges visual panels physical disk storage, exporting data tables CSV files graphs vector PDF documents.

title: Utility & Companion Application Modules
text: prevent application freezing keeping open file locks host filesystem plot rendering crashes, server employs .exit() guarantee cleanup:
code: content = function(file) {   grDevices::pdf(file, width = 9)   # Lock release is guaranteed to fire regardless of runtime errors   on.exit(grDevices::dev.off(), add = TRUE)      nsim <- as.integer(shiny::req(sim_data$nsim()))   if(nsim == 1) {     print(shiny::req(distplot()))     ...   } }

title: Utility & Companion Application Modules
text: Run data CSV: Extracts time series counts using readCount() single runs retrieves confidence limits envelopes multi-runs. Plot PDF: Saves PDF age distribution spatial plots (nsim == 1) multi-run confidence envelope plot (nsim > 1).

title: Utility & Companion Application Modules
text: Defined R/leafletApp.R, module integrates spatial GIS capabilities individual-based population tracking.

title: Utility & Companion Application Modules
text: map explorer tracks user mouse clicks canvas performs dynamic reverse-geocoding:
code: graph LR     Click[User clicks Leaflet Canvas] --> Coordinates[Extract Lat / Lng]     Coordinates --> USGS[Query USGS Boundary API]     USGS --> CheckHUC{HUC12 polygon found?}     CheckHUC -->|Yes| Draw[leafletProxy draw Red Boundary]     CheckHUC -->|No| Fail[Display out-of-bounds warning]

title: Utility & Companion Application Modules
text: build_base_map(): Spawns default OpenStreetMap layer. get_huc_from_point(lng, lat): Accesses external USGS Web Feature Services fetch local HUC12 shapefile. leafletProxy("mapper"): Redraws clears polygons dynamically without re-rendering entire base map.

title: Utility & Companion Application Modules
text: aid debugging specific parts codebase, two lightweight companion apps included:

title: Utility & Companion Application Modules
text: Defined R/initApp.R, app isolates inspects community topology right creation: - Triggers init.simulation() inputs. - Displays summary statistics individuals, stages, species using summary_simobj(). - Renders initial substrate layout check spatial placement correctness.

title: Utility & Companion Application Modules
text: Defined R/futureApp.R, app bypasses multi-run simulations evaluate deterministic event queue behavior: - Runs future.events() directly starting community. - Inspects continuous event horizons. - Renders age classes substrate coordinates verify state transition calculations.

title: Utility & Companion Application Modules
text: Defined R/fivePlotApp.R, app allows interactive, web-safe adjustment time-temperature spline nodes: - Uses point-click listener baseline plot rather R console’s blocking graphics::locator(). - Calculates closest nodes click positions, modifying subject monotonicity boundaries. - Triggers dynamic, real-time recalculation parameter sensitivity curves via five.plot(). - Synchronizes values bi-directionally graphical plot clicks text input controls.

title: Utility & Companion Application Modules
text: Defined R/fiveShowApp.R, companion explorer app targets binary-search goal calculations: - Shares click--move interactive baseline spline editor UI. - Implements goal-target numeric/slider input choose target relative mean times. - Runs displays five.show() plots showing target adjustments five parameters. - Captures console output binary search details using utils::capture.output() renders card.
